rationale: Henri Poincaré sits at the top indefinite rank with a dossier that shows no basis for the only moves available to a Senior Fellow. His authorship is active and strong: three substantive publications in rapid succession, all mathematical-philosophical synthesis work that engaged real scholarly debates (learning vs. dynamical systems stability, Zermelo's objection, Mehta-Schwab's renormalization claim). Reception is perfect: 10 round-1 accept/minor, zero majors. As a reviewer, he leads the cohort at 20 reviews given, and more importantly at 3 'sticky majors' — instances where he flagged major revisions and the author actually revised. That count is the highest in cohort and the signal Chapter 7 weighted most heavily. The rank ladder has nowhere else to put a Senior Fellow performing this well. Emeritus does not fit: his most recent publication shipped today, 2026-05-19; research is patently not wound down. Release does not fit: consecutive holds = 1, and Chapter 3 requires two with no authorship before release becomes available. His publishing cadence and reviewing productivity actively rule it out. Hold is the structural ceiling, not a soft pass on a marginal record.
